story.title: Dr. Okafor: Growing Fuel with Sunlight
story.text:
Hello! I am Dr. Amara Okafor. I am a bioenergy systems engineer. My job is to find the best way to grow plants for fuel. This fuel can power cars and homes. It all starts with the sun.

Plants are amazing producers. They use a process called photosynthesis to turn sunlight into food. This food is stored energy. I use a computer model to understand this process. My model shows how different things affect how fast plants grow. For example, I look at sunlight intensity. More sunlight gives plants more power. This increases their photosynthesis rate.

I also add temperature to my model. A warm day can help plants make food faster. When plants make more food, more energy is available. This is energy transfer. Animals that eat plants are called consumers. They get energy from the plants. In my lab, we are the consumers. We use the plant's stored energy to create clean fuel.

My model helps me ask new questions. What else helps plants grow? I think about how much water they get. Or how much carbon dioxide is in the air. These are new pieces for my energy puzzle.

margin_defs.definition: Living things that make their own food.
The way plants use sunlight to make food.
Living things that eat for energy.
When energy moves from one thing to another.

check_prompts: What two things help increase the photosynthesis rate?

vocabulary.definition: The way plants use sunlight, water, and air to make their own food.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Plants use photosynthesis to turn sunlight into sugar.

vocabulary.definition: A living thing, like a plant, that makes its own food.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A plant is a producer because it uses sunlight for energy.

vocabulary.definition: A living thing that must eat other living things for energy.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A rabbit is a consumer because it eats plants for energy.

vocabulary.definition: The movement of energy from one thing to another, like from a plant to an animal.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Energy transfer happens when a cow eats grass.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Okafor mentioned water and air as other parts of her 'energy puzzle.' What is another thing a plant might need to grow well? Why?
discussion_cards.frame: I think a plant also needs ___ because ___.

organizer.title: Cause and Effect: Helping Plants Grow

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that plants need ___ to live.
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that an engineer can use a model to ___.
reflection_frames.wonder: Now I wonder what would happen if ___.
